children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be in the Department of Homeland Security a bureau to be known as the “Bureau of Border Security”.

children.label: A
children.text: shall report directly to the Under Secretary for Border and Transportation Security; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall have a minimum of 5 years professional experience in law enforcement, and a minimum of 5 years of management experience.

children.label: i
children.text: transferred to the Under Secretary for Border and Transportation Security by and delegated to the Assistant Secretary by the Under Secretary for Border and Transportation Security; orsection 251 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise vested in the Assistant Secretary by law;

children.label: B
children.text: shall oversee the administration of such policies; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall advise the Under Secretary for Border and Transportation Security with respect to any policy or operation of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement affecting U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services established under part E of this subchapter, including potentially conflicting policies or operations.

children.heading: Program to collect information relating to foreign students
children.label: 4 Program to collect information relating to foreign students
children.text: The Assistant Secretary of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement shall be responsible for administering the program to collect information relating to nonimmigrant foreign students and other exchange program participants described in , including the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System established under that section, and shall use such information to carry out the enforcement functions of the agency.section 1372 of title 8

children.label: i
children.text: gain some experience in all the major functions performed by such agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: work in at least one local office of such agency.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which the transfer of functions specified under takes effect, the Secretary shall submit a report to the Congress on the implementation of such program.section 251 of this title

children.heading: Chief of Policy and Strategy
children.label: b Chief of Policy and Strategy

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be a position of Chief of Policy and Strategy for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

children.label: A
children.text: making policy recommendations and performing policy research and analysis on immigration enforcement issues; and

children.label: B
children.text: coordinating immigration policy issues with the Chief of Policy and Strategy for U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (established under part E of this subchapter), as appropriate.

children.heading: Legal advisor
children.label: c Legal advisor
children.text: There shall be a principal legal advisor to the Assistant Secretary of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The legal advisor shall provide specialized legal advice to the Assistant Secretary of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement and shall represent the agency in all exclusion, deportation, and removal proceedings before the Executive Office for Immigration Review.
